pi_items.source: Verified in setup_task.sh line 29 and export_result.sh line 7
Verified in setup_task.sh line 174 (CSV header row)

Web search confirmed: Kardégic is the official pharmaceutical brand name (acetylsalicylic acid/aspirin) manufactured by Sanofi for cardiovascular prevention. Spelling with accent on 'e' is correct. See: https://www.substipharm.com/news/paris-31-october-2025-substipharm-strengthens-its-portfolio-with-the-acquisition-of-iconic-brands-aspegic-from-opella-and-kardegic-cardirenefrom-sanofi/

Web search confirmed: Sintrom (also branded as Sinthrome in UK) is a valid pharmaceutical brand name for acenocoumarol, an oral anticoagulant. 'Sintrom' spelling is correct and commonly used. See: https://medbroadcast.com/drug/getdrug/sintrom and https://www.medicines.org.uk/emc/product/2058/smpc

Web search confirmed serious drug-drug interaction between aspirin and anticoagulants. Meta-analysis shows 2-4x increase in bleeding complications (gastrointestinal, intracranial, excessive minor bleeding). See: https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamainternalmedicine/fullarticle/2726050 and https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38263263/

privileged_info_summary: The task requires identifying patients from a synthetic DrTuxTest database who have BOTH Kardégic (aspirin) and Sintrom (acenocoumarol) mentioned in their clinical history. Both drugs are real pharmaceuticals with a serious documented drug-drug interaction (2-4x increased bleeding risk when combined). The synthetic dataset contains 20+ procedurally-generated patients divided into 4 groups: 4 with both drugs (targets), 5 with only Kardégic, 5 with only Sintrom, and remainder with neither. The ground truth file lists exactly 4 target patients with GUIDs. The task schema uses MedinTux database tables (IndexNomPrenom, fchpat, Rubriques) for patient data and clinical notes. Output must be a CSV at /home/ga/Documents/interaction_alert_list.csv with columns PatientName and GUID.
